summary: Implement M6 desktop slicer handoff through a fresh full-quality 3MF export and a bounded native temp-file/process port.

decisions.decision: Keep 3MF generation in the existing EngineService export boundary and give the desktop adapter only the completed bytes, a safe suggested name, and an optional explicit executable path. The browser composition exposes no handoff service.
decisions.rationale: FR-15.10 requires a real full-quality export and desktop process launch. Separating those responsibilities avoids a second engine/export path and prevents shared UI code from gaining file-system or process authority.

decisions.decision: Prefer a user-supplied absolute existing executable; otherwise inspect only bounded well-known Program Files and LocalAppData locations for PrusaSlicer, OrcaSlicer, Cura, and Bambu Studio. Write with create-new semantics under the ScadMill temp namespace, cap decoded 3MF data at 512 MiB, and remove only handoff-owned files older than seven days.
decisions.rationale: Passive bounded detection meets the desktop workflow without registry mutation, broad disk search, executable bundling, accidental overwrite, or unbounded temp accumulation.

testEvidence.criterion: A slicer handoff must perform an ordinary 3MF export and must not launch on export failure.
testEvidence.test: Drive the application service with a spy EngineService and SlicerHandoffPort, inspect the exact export request and launch bytes, then fail export and cancel the active job.

red.outcome: RED: neither the application slicer-handoff service nor the native adapter existed.

testEvidence.criterion: The desktop adapter must use bounded passive detection, safe unique temp files, strict transport, and explicit browser unavailability.
testEvidence.test: Exercise configured-path precedence, detection order including versioned installs, unique non-overwriting 3MF writes, desktop/web capability composition, and strict Tauri response decoding.

red.outcome: RED was established before implementation: no native slicer module or command was registered.

green.command: cargo test slicer_handoff --locked; pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/platform-desktop/desktop-platform.test.ts tests/platform-web/web-platform.test.ts tests/platform-desktop/tauri-slicer-handoff.test.ts
green.outcome: GREEN: 4 Rust slicer tests passed; the combined TypeScript handoff/platform/UI slice passed 15 of 15 tests, and the final affected UI rerun passed 7 of 7 tests.

testEvidence.criterion: The Manufacturing UI must distinguish desktop availability, support an explicit executable, and avoid premature multi-object filament claims.
testEvidence.test: Open the Manufacturing activity through the real Workbench, run 3MF export into the typed handoff port, and assert the unavailable and multi-object honesty states.

red.outcome: RED: the slicer executable input, Open in slicer action, desktop-only state, and outcome copy were absent.

green.command: pnpm.cmd run typecheck; pnpm.cmd run lint; pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/manufacturing/ManufacturingActivity.test.tsx tests/ui/Workbench.manufacturing.test.tsx
green.outcome: GREEN: TypeScript passed, lint/source policy checked 586 files, and the affected UI suites passed 7 of 7 tests.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
